title: Quicksilver Schema Metamodel
description: JSON Schema metamodel for quicksilver entity and edge tables. Generates: (1) Spark StructType definitions in Scala, (2) Neo4j 5.21+ type/property constraints. Each $def is a table schema; 'required' = source must provide the column (Spark nullable=false), absent from required = optional/null-filled.

sparkTypeAnnotation.description: Custom vocabulary: x-spark-type maps JSON Schema types to Spark DataTypes where the mapping is ambiguous (e.g. integer → IntegerType vs LongType). x-neo4j-type provides the Neo4j property type.

entityBase.description: Base columns present on every entity table.

catalog.description: Origin catalog, set by QuicksilverConsolidation. Optional so source pipelines can omit it.

edgeBase.description: Base columns present on every edge table.

committee_entities.x-description: Political committees (PACs, party committees, campaign committees)

office_candidate_stats.x-description: Aggregate candidate stats per office (does not use standard entityBase)

block_entities.x-description: Census blocks with voter registration and turnout data

catalog_meta_entities.x-description: Meta-entity representing a Unity Catalog catalog

table_meta_entities.x-description: Meta-entity representing a quicksilver table within a catalog

field_meta_entities.x-description: Meta-entity representing a field across catalogs

source_id_entities.x-description: Source-specific identifier entities for cross-catalog resolution

candidate_name_vertices.x-description: Name-match vertices for candidate entity resolution

committee_name_vertices.x-description: Name-match vertices for committee entity resolution

x-resolution-rules.description: Civitech per-state voter tables resolve to the base voter_entities schema

x-neo4j-limitations: Neo4j 5 cannot constrain edge endpoints — (Individual)-[PAID]->(Individual) is technically allowed even though the schema says PAID goes Committee->Vendor
Enforce endpoint constraints at write time via application code or APOC triggers
Node/relationship type constraints are union-style — one constraint must list ALL permitted labels/types
